_dataNotes: Data updated June 22, 2026. Groups G & H matchday 2 (June 21) now fully reflected: Spain 4-0 Saudi Arabia (Yamal 10', Oyarzabal 21'&24', Al-Tambakti OG 51'), Belgium 0-0 Iran (Ngoy red card 66'), Uruguay 2-2 Cabo Verde (Pina 21', Araújo 44', Canobbio 45+6', H. Varela 61'), New Zealand 1-3 Egypt (Surman 15', Ziko 58', Salah 67', Trezeguet 82'). Spain and Egypt top their groups. Group I–L matchday 2 (June 22–23) not yet played.

tournament.name: FIFA World Cup 2026
tournament.format: 48 teams, 12 groups of 4. Top 2 from each group plus 8 best 3rd-place teams advance to Round of 32. Then knockout rounds through to the Final.

highlights: Julián Quiñones scored the tournament opening goal in the 9th minute — first CONCACAF player to score the opening goal of a World Cup
Raúl Jiménez added a second in the 67th minute
Mexico opened the tournament as co-host in front of a packed Azteca
Krejčí gave Czechia the lead in the 59th minute
Hwang In-beom equalized for South Korea in the 67th minute
Oh Hyeon-gyu scored the winner in the 80th minute
Own goal opened the scoring in the 7th minute after Pulisic and McKennie pressure
Folarin Balogun scored twice — first American with 2 WC goals in a game since 1930
Paraguay pulled one back through Mauricio in the 73rd minute
Gio Reyna sealed it with a stunning stoppage-time strike
Australia controlled the match throughout
Turkey failed to register a shot on target
Canada drew their opening home World Cup match in front of a passionate Toronto crowd
Vinicius Junior scored for Brazil
Brazil held to a draw by Morocco in a heavyweight opening clash
Result let Scotland take the Group C lead
Scotland won their first World Cup match since 1974
Scotland's first World Cup appearance in 28 years (since France 1998)
Shock result puts Scotland top of Group C above Brazil and Morocco
Qatar earned their first-ever World Cup point
Qatar had gone winless as 2022 hosts, exiting in the group stage
Germany became the all-time top scorers in World Cup history (239 goals), surpassing Brazil
Curacao — smallest nation ever at a men's World Cup (pop. ~155,000) — scored their first-ever World Cup goal
Kai Havertz scored twice; Jamal Musiala also on the scoresheet
Amad Diallo scored the winner in the 90th minute from a Wilfried Singo assist
Ecuador hit the woodwork twice in the first half
Late drama as Ivory Coast snatched all three points in stoppage time
Van Dijk (50') and Summerville (64') scored for the Netherlands
Nakamura (57') and Kamada (88') scored for Japan
Japan equalized in the 88th minute through Daichi Kamada
Attendance: 69,285
Yasin Ayari scored twice including a late long-range stunner
Viktor Gyökeres and Alexander Isak starred for Sweden
Svanberg's goal confirmed by VAR after a lengthy review (84')
Spain held to a shock 0-0 draw by tournament debutants Cabo Verde
Cabo Verde (pop. ~530,000) earned a famous result against World Cup favorites Spain
Attendance: 67,640
Al-Amri scored for Saudi Arabia in the 41st minute
M. Araújo equalized for Uruguay in the 80th minute
Attendance: 62,764
Belgium and Egypt played out a 1-1 draw
Iran and New Zealand played out a 2-2 thriller
Elijah Just scored for New Zealand
Kylian Mbappé became France's all-time top scorer, surpassing Olivier Giroud (58 goals in 99 caps)
Mbappé scored twice (66', 90+6'); Bradley Barcola added one (82')
Michael Olise provided two assists
Ibrahim Mbaye scored a late consolation for Senegal (90+5')
Erling Haaland scored a brace on his World Cup debut — Norway's first World Cup since 1998
Haaland's second came after the Iraqi keeper accidentally booted the ball into him
Iraq scored their first World Cup goal since 1986
Lionel Messi scored a hat trick — his first career World Cup hat trick — tying Miroslav Klose's all-time WC record of 16 goals
Messi became the oldest player to score a World Cup hat trick (age 38)
Defending champions Argentina opened their title defense in dominant fashion
Messi was substituted off in the 80th minute after securing his hat trick
Romano Schmid opened the scoring in the 21st minute with a long-range right-foot strike
Ali Iyad Olwan equalized for Jordan in the 50th minute — Jordan's first-ever World Cup goal
Yazan Al-Arab own goal (76') restored Austria's lead after a corner deflection
Marco Arnautović converted a stoppage-time penalty to seal 3-1 — Austria's first World Cup win in 36 years
João Neves opened the scoring for Portugal in the 6th minute
Yoane Wissa equalized for DR Congo in the 45+5th minute — DR Congo's first World Cup point since returning after 52 years (last appeared 1974)
João Cancelo's spectacular overhead kick in the 55th minute ruled out by VAR for offside
Portugal frustrated by debutants; Ronaldo started at age 41 as tournament's oldest outfield player
Daniel Muñoz scored for Colombia in the 40th minute
Abbosbek Fayzullaev equalized for Uzbekistan in the 60th minute — Uzbekistan's first-ever World Cup goal
Luis Díaz restored Colombia's lead in the 65th minute (goal and assist on the night)
Jáminton Campaz headed home in the 90+9th minute to seal 3-1 and put Colombia top of Group K
Harry Kane scored twice (12' pen, 42' header) to reach 10 England World Cup goals — equalling Gary Lineker's national record
Croatia hit back through Martin Baturina (36') and Petar Musa (45+5') to level 2-2 at half-time
Jude Bellingham drove in to restore England's lead immediately after the break (47')
Marcus Rashford sealed it with a composed finish (85'); England's dominant second half secured a 4-2 win
Caleb Yirenkyi tapped home from Brandon Thomas-Asante's cross in the 95th minute for a dramatic winner
Panama's disciplined defensive display was undone in stoppage time, denying them a historic first World Cup point
Ghana join England at the top of Group L on 3 points after matchday 1
Michal Sadilek opened the scoring for Czech Republic in the 6th minute
Pavel Šulc's handball inside the box in the 81st minute conceded a penalty to South Africa
Teboho Mokoena converted the penalty in the 83rd minute to earn South Africa their first World Cup point
Czech Republic failed to win despite taking the lead, both teams level on 1 point in Group A
Johan Manzambi scored just 171 seconds after being introduced as a substitute in the 71st minute
Tarik Muharemovic was shown a straight red card, leaving Bosnia with 10 men
Manzambi added a second (90'), Ruben Vargas scored (84'), and Granit Xhaka converted a penalty (90+7')
Switzerland's four-goal second-half burst puts them top of Group B
Jonathan David scored a hat trick (29', 45+3', 90+2') — Canada's first-ever World Cup win
Cyle Larin (16'), Nathan Saliba (64'), and Mohamed Al Manai own goal (75') completed the rout
Qatar had two players sent off and finished with nine men
Biggest win by a CONCACAF team in men's World Cup history
Luis Romo scored in the 50th minute after South Korea goalkeeper Kim Seung-gyu collided with defender Lee Gi-hyuk and dropped the ball
Mexico became the first team to qualify for the Round of 32 — clinching Group A top spot with two wins
Goalkeeper Raúl Rangel made two outstanding saves in the 87th minute to deny Cho Gue-sung and Yang Hyun-jun
Mexico win Group A with 6 points from two games; South Korea drop to third-place battle
Cameron Burgess own goal in the 11th minute — Balogun's cross forced the Australian defender to turn in
Alex Freeman headed home in the 43rd minute from Sergiño Dest's free-kick; VAR confirmed after a lengthy offside review
USMNT advance to Round of 32 with 6 points from two wins; Pulisic absent through injury
Ismael Saibari scored just 70 seconds after kick-off — the fastest winning goal in 2026 World Cup history
Saibari collected Brahim Diaz's through ball and slammed past Angus Gunn into the roof of the net
McTominay denied a controversial late penalty; Scotland unable to level
Morocco top Group C on goal difference ahead of Brazil; Scotland slip to 2nd
Matheus Cunha tapped home in the 23rd minute after Vinicius Jr's saved effort
Cunha added a composed left-footed finish in the 36th minute for his brace
Vinicius Junior completed the rout in the 45+3rd minute after Lucas Paquetá's through ball
Haiti become the first team eliminated from the 2026 World Cup; Brazil level with Morocco on 4 points, lead on GD
Matías Galarza struck two minutes in — the fastest goal of the tournament so far
Miguel Almirón received a straight red card in first-half stoppage time under FIFA's new mouth-covering rule
Ten-man Paraguay held firm through Turkey's relentless second-half pressure to claim a vital 1-0 win
Turkey eliminated after two consecutive defeats; Paraguay's Round of 32 hopes remain alive
Franck Kessié gave Ivory Coast a shock lead with a 30th-minute strike against the tournament's top scorers
Deniz Undav came off the bench to equalise in the 68th minute with a volley from Amiri's cross
Undav struck again in the 94th minute to complete a stunning comeback — Germany advance to the Round of 32
Ivory Coast remain in contention; a high-quality display despite the defeat
Goalkeeper Eloy Room made 15 saves to earn Curaçao their first-ever World Cup point
Ecuador had 15 shots on target without scoring — the most in a World Cup match without a goal since 1966
One of the great World Cup goalkeeping performances; Room was the undisputed man of the match
Curaçao earn a historic point; Ecuador's Group E knockout hopes now in serious doubt
Brian Brobbey scored twice inside 17 minutes (5', 17') — the fourth-fastest brace in World Cup history
Cody Gakpo added two more in the second half (47', 54'); Anthony Elanga pulled one back for Sweden (59')
Crysencio Summerville completed the rout with a fifth in the 89th minute
Netherlands' biggest-ever World Cup win; they move joint-top of Group F with Japan
Daichi Kamada struck in the 4th minute to give Japan an immediate lead
Ayase Ueda scored twice (30', 83') and Junya Ito added Japan's third in the 69th minute
This was the 1,000th match in FIFA World Cup history — Japan marked it with their biggest-ever World Cup winning margin
Tunisia eliminated from the tournament; Japan join Netherlands at the top of Group F on 4 points
Lamine Yamal scored his first World Cup goal in the 10th minute at age 17 — the 8th-youngest scorer in World Cup history, surpassing Messi, Mbappé and Ronaldo's age at their first WC goals
Mikel Oyarzabal added a brace in under three minutes (21' & 24') to make it 3-0 before half-time
Yamal and Oyarzabal were both substituted at half-time with Spain already in full control
Hassan Al-Tambakti scored an own goal just after the interval (51') to complete a 4-0 rout — Spain top Group H and advance to the Round of 32
Mehdi Taremi had a goal ruled out by VAR for offside after a well-worked free kick in the first half
Nathan Ngoy was sent off in the 66th minute after miskicking a back-pass then fouling Taremi who was clean through on goal
Belgium had 70% possession and 1.82 xG but could not break down Iran's resolute defense even with a man advantage
Alireza Beiranvand made crucial saves as Iran held 10-man Belgium to a goalless draw — all four Group G teams remain in knockout contention
Kevin Pina opened the scoring for Cabo Verde with a powerful free kick in the 21st minute
Maximiliano Araújo equalized for Uruguay in the 44th minute and Agustín Canobbio struck in first-half stoppage time (45+6') to give Uruguay a 2-1 lead
Hélio Varela levelled for Cabo Verde in the 61st minute after a defensive error to make it 2-2
Cabo Verde remain unbeaten at the 2026 World Cup with two draws — both teams still in knockout contention heading into matchday 3
Finn Surman headed in from a corner in the 15th minute to give New Zealand an early lead
Ziko equalized for Egypt with a close-range header in the 58th minute to level at 1-1
Mohamed Salah scored the go-ahead goal in the 67th minute as Egypt's comeback gathered momentum
Trezeguet sealed Egypt's first-ever World Cup win with a diving header in the 82nd minute — Egypt move top of Group G

storylines: Lionel Messi scored a hat trick vs Algeria (June 16) — his first WC hat trick — tying Miroslav Klose's all-time record of 16 World Cup goals. At 38, he is the oldest player to score a WC hat trick.
Kylian Mbappé became France's all-time top international scorer (surpassing Olivier Giroud) with a brace vs Senegal, reaching 58 goals in 99 caps.
Scotland — back at the World Cup for the first time since 1998 — sit top of Group C above Brazil and Morocco after beating Haiti 1-0.
Cabo Verde (pop. ~530,000) held favorite Spain to a 0-0 draw on their tournament debut — one of the biggest shocks of the opening week.
Curacao became the smallest nation in men's World Cup history (pop. ~155,000) and scored their first-ever World Cup goal against Germany despite losing 7-1.
Erling Haaland made his World Cup debut with a brace in Norway's 4-1 win over Iraq — Norway's first World Cup since 1998.
Germany became the all-time top scorers in World Cup history (239 goals), surpassing Brazil, with their 7-1 rout of Curacao.
Folarin Balogun became the first American to score two goals in a World Cup game since the inaugural 1930 tournament, in the USMNT's 4-1 win over Paraguay.
Qatar earned their first-ever World Cup point with a 1-1 draw against Switzerland, having gone 0-3 as 2022 hosts.
Julián Quiñones scored the tournament's opening goal for Mexico — the first CONCACAF player to score a World Cup opener.
Italy missed out on the tournament — the first former champion to miss three consecutive World Cups.
This is the first World Cup co-hosted by three nations, the first with 48 teams, and the first in North America since 1994.
As of June 16, 19 matches had produced 58 goals (3.05 per match) with average attendance of 65,322.
Austria defeated Jordan 3-1 on June 17 — Austria's first World Cup win since 1990 (36 years). Jordan made their World Cup debut and scored their first-ever World Cup goal through Ali Iyad Olwan.
England beat Croatia 4-2 in their Group L opener on June 17 — Harry Kane scored twice to reach 10 England World Cup goals, equalling Gary Lineker's national record.
Colombia topped Group K after a 3-1 win over Uzbekistan at the Estadio Azteca on June 17. Abbosbek Fayzullaev scored Uzbekistan's first-ever World Cup goal.
Ghana's Caleb Yirenkyi scored a 95th-minute winner to beat Panama 1-0 in Group L on June 17 — Ghana join England at the top on 3 points.
Portugal were held to a 1-1 draw by DR Congo on June 17 — Yoane Wissa's 45+5 equalizer earned DR Congo their first World Cup point since their only previous appearance in 1974.

Jonathan David scored a hat trick as Canada beat Qatar 6-0 on June 18 — Canada's first-ever World Cup win and the biggest margin of victory by a CONCACAF team in men's World Cup history. Qatar had two players sent off.

Mexico became the first team to clinch a place in the Round of 32, defeating South Korea 1-0 on June 18 thanks to Luis Romo's 50th-minute goal after a goalkeeper error. Mexico top Group A with 6 points from 2 games.

Switzerland's Johan Manzambi scored twice as a substitute in a stunning 4-1 comeback win over Bosnia and Herzegovina on June 18 (trailing 0-0 at 70 mins). Switzerland top Group B level on points with Canada.
USA beat Australia 2-0 on June 19 (Burgess OG 11', Freeman header 43') — USMNT advance to Round of 32 with 6 points from two games. Christian Pulisic missed the match through injury.
Morocco beat Scotland 1-0 with Ismael Saibari's goal after just 70 seconds — the fastest winning goal in 2026 World Cup history. Morocco top Group C on goal difference ahead of Brazil.
Brazil crushed Haiti 3-0 on June 19 (Cunha 23', 36'; Vinicius Jr 45+3') — Haiti become the first team eliminated from the 2026 World Cup.
Paraguay beat Turkey 1-0 on June 19 despite having Miguel Almirón sent off under FIFA's new mouth-covering rule — Galarza's 2nd-minute strike was enough; Turkey eliminated after two straight defeats.

Deniz Undav scored twice off the bench as Germany came from behind to beat Ivory Coast 2-1 on June 20 (Kessié 30' for CIV; Undav 68', 94') — Germany advance to the Round of 32 with a 94th-minute winner.
Curaçao earned their first-ever World Cup point on June 20 with a 0-0 draw against Ecuador — goalkeeper Eloy Room made 15 saves in one of the great individual World Cup goalkeeping performances.

Netherlands crushed Sweden 5-1 on June 20 (Brobbey 5',17'; Gakpo 47',54'; Summerville 89') — their biggest-ever World Cup win. Brian Brobbey's two goals inside 17 minutes were the fourth-fastest brace in World Cup history.

Japan defeated Tunisia 4-0 on June 21 (Kamada 4'; Ueda 30',83'; Ito 69') in the 1,000th match in World Cup history — Japan's biggest-ever World Cup winning margin; they join Netherlands at the top of Group F.

Lamine Yamal (17) scored his first World Cup goal as Spain thrashed Saudi Arabia 4-0 on June 21 in Atlanta (Yamal 10', Oyarzabal 21'&24', Al-Tambakti OG 51'). Yamal becomes the 8th-youngest scorer in World Cup history, surpassing Messi, Mbappé and Ronaldo's debut WC goal ages. Spain top Group H and advance to the Round of 32.

Egypt claimed their first-ever World Cup win on June 21, beating New Zealand 3-1 in Vancouver (Surman 15' for NZL; Ziko 58', Salah 67', Trezeguet 82'). Mohamed Salah was decisive as Egypt move top of Group G with 4 points.

Belgium and Iran drew 0-0 on June 21 in Los Angeles — Belgium finished with 10 men after Nathan Ngoy's red card (66' for fouling Taremi). Taremi also had a goal chalked off by VAR. All four Group G teams remain in knockout contention heading into matchday 3.

Uruguay and Cabo Verde drew 2-2 on June 21 in Miami (Pina 21', Araújo 44', Canobbio 45+6', H. Varela 61'). Cabo Verde remain unbeaten at the 2026 World Cup; Group H's bottom three are separated by just one point.
